questions.explanation: `len()` returns the number of elements in a list; `[1,2,3]` has 3 elements.
questions.question: In Python, what does the expression `len([1, 2, 3])` return?

questions.explanation: The binomial distribution models the count of successes across n independent trials with success probability p.
questions.question: Which probability distribution is commonly used to model the number of successes in a fixed number of independent Bernoulli trials?
In linear algebra, what is the dot product of vectors a = [1, 2] and b = [3, 4]?

questions.explanation: Supervised learning uses labeled examples: features (X) with targets/labels (y).

options.C: Input features paired with correct labels
options.D: Randomly generated synthetic data without targets

questions.question: In supervised machine learning, what best describes the training data?

questions.explanation: Standardization is done per feature/column, so compute mean and std along axis=0.
questions.question: You have a NumPy array `X` with shape (1000, 20). You want to standardize each feature to zero mean and unit variance. Which operation is correct (ignoring numerical stability)?

questions.explanation: One-hot encoding represents categories without introducing ordinal relationships that can mislead linear models.

options.B: One-hot encoding with three binary columns
options.C: Hashing into a single integer feature
options.D: Min-max scaling of the string values

questions.question: A dataset has a categorical feature `color` with values {red, green, blue} and a binary target. You plan to use logistic regression. Which encoding is generally most appropriate to avoid imposing an artificial order?

questions.explanation: With severe class imbalance, accuracy can be misleading; precision/recall (and F1) better reflect detection quality for the minority class.
questions.question: You are evaluating a binary classifier on a highly imbalanced dataset (1% positive). Accuracy is 99%, but the model rarely detects positives. Which metric is typically more informative for the positive class performance?

questions.explanation: Diverging train/validation performance indicates overfitting; early stopping and stronger regularization help improve generalization.

options.C: Apply early stopping or increase regularization
options.D: Train for more epochs without changes

questions.question: You are training a deep network and observe training loss decreasing while validation loss increases over epochs. Which intervention most directly targets this issue?

questions.explanation: `DistributedSampler` partitions data per rank; calling `set_epoch` changes the shuffle seed each epoch so shards differ and remain synchronized across processes.

options.C: Disable shuffling entirely to avoid overlap
options.D: Manually split the dataset once and never reshuffle

questions.question: In PyTorch distributed data parallel (DDP) training across multiple GPUs, what is the correct way to ensure each process sees a different shard of the dataset each epoch?

questions.explanation: A controlled experiment varies one factor (optimizer) while holding others constant and uses multiple seeds to account for stochasticity, enabling a fair causal comparison.

options.A: Change the optimizer and also change the model architecture to match each optimizer’s strengths
options.B: Hold the dataset, preprocessing, model, and training budget constant; vary only the optimizer and run multiple random seeds
options.C: Pick the best result from many hyperparameter searches for one optimizer, but use default settings for the other
options.D: Evaluate on the training set only to avoid test leakage

questions.question: You are writing a research proposal to compare two optimization methods for training a model. Which experimental design choice best improves causal interpretability of the comparison?
